explanations.24: Promise.all waits for all the async operations (the map calls) to finish before returning their results as an array.
explanations.32: The .flat() method flattens nested arrays into a single list, so if some entries returned [path1] and some returned [], they all merge into one array.
explanations.42: This function returns an object (not called immediately); Docusaurus will call the returned object's postBuild method after building the site.
explanations.70: Promise.allSettled runs all file updates in parallel and captures both successes and failures instead of stopping at the first error.
explanations.82: The replace method here uses a function callback that receives the matched text (m) so we can insert the banner right after the doctype tag.
explanations.90: Writing to a temporary file first, then renaming it, is safer because the rename is atomic (all-or-nothing), preventing other processes from reading incomplete data.
explanations.101: This filters for results where the outer Promise settled successfully (fulfilled) and the inner operation also succeeded (status ok).
